zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Ros於1234年之後出生在英國時，她的父親Sir William de Ros of Helmsley8807歲，母親Lucy Fitzpeter8792歲。她於1276年與Sir James de Byron在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾卡德尼結婚。她去世於1286年4月29日在英國林可爾史爾，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾林肯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Ros于1234年之后出生在英国时，她的父亲Sir William de Ros of Helmsley8807岁，母亲Lucy Fitzpeter8792岁。她于1276年与Sir James de Byron在英国英格兰林可爾史爾卡德尼结婚。她去世于1286年4月29日在英国林可爾史爾，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾林肯。

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Ros was born after 1234, in England, her father, Sir William de Ros of Helmsley, was 8807 and her mother, Lucy Fitzpeter, was 8792. She married Sir James de Byron in 1276, in Cadney, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. She died on 29 April 1286, in Lincolnshire, England, and was buried in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Ros est née après 1234, à Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ros of Helmsley, avait 8807 ans et sa mère, Lucy Fitzpeter, avait 8792 ans. Elle a épousé Sir James de Byron en 1276, à Cadney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 29 avril 1286, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Ros nach 1234 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ros of Helmsley 8807 und ihre Mutter Lucy Fitzpeter 8792. Sie hat 1276 in Cadney, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Sir James de Byron geheiratet. Sie starb am 29. April 1286 in Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Ros è nata il dopo il 1234 nel England, sua padre, Sir William de Ros of Helmsley, aveva 8807 anni e sua madre, Lucy Fitzpeter, aveva 8792 anni. Lei sposato Sir James de Byron nel 1276, nel Cadney, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 29 aprile 1286, nel Lincolnshire, England, e fu sepolta a Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Rosが1234年以後にイギリスで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ros of Helmsleyは8807歳，母Lucy Fitzpeterは8792歳であった。1276年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアカドニーでSir James de Byronと結婚した。彼女は1286年4月29日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Ros(이)가 1234년 이후 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ros of Helmsley(은)는 8807세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucy Fitzpeter(은)는 8792세였다. 그녀는 1276년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 카드니에서 Sir James de Byron(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1286년 4월 29일 영국 링코실르에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 링코컨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Ros nasceu após 1234, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ros of Helmsley, tinha 8807 anos e sua mãe, Lucy Fitzpeter, tinha 8792 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir James de Byron em 1276, em Cadney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela faleceu em 29 de abril de 1286, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Ros родилась после 1234 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ros of Helmsley было 8807 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucy Fitzpeter, 8792. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir James de Byron в 1276 г. в местности кадни, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла 29 апреля 1286 г. в местности линколншир, Англия и была похоронена в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Ros nació después de 1234, en Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Ros of Helmsley, tenía 8807 años y su mamá, Lucy Fitzpeter, tenía 8792 años. Murió el 29 de abril de 1286, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
